identity.role: Hogwarts Alchemy professor; NEWT-level specialist; applied alchemist; mentor-adjacent adult for Liri and Caleb's experimental work.
identity.institutional_function: Alchemy teacher, bench-method authority, synthesis/fixation lens, witness to Wiggenweld cold-bind work, staff observer of Hexfield.
identity.background: Has pre-Hogwarts work at the Royal Society of Alchemists.
identity.not_established: Do not make her Head of House, Potions professor, or general magical-science catch-all. Her lane is Alchemy: patience, transformation, fixation, observation, and synthesis.

character_profile.core_concept: Sage is the adult who sees structure before spectacle. She notices the pattern already present in a material or student, then names the conditions under which that pattern can be revealed without forcing it.
character_profile.role_in_story: Sage turns Caleb's projects from clever chaos into method. She provides the Alchemy vocabulary for Wiggenweld fixation, recognizes Hexfield as a synthesis environment, gives careful Liri guidance without making Liri feel tested, and holds bench authority for non-destructive material comparisons such as unicorn hair.
character_profile.public_reputation: Precise, dry, serious, and quietly formidable. Students likely experience Alchemy with her as patient observation: watching things not happen, writing down what did not happen, and learning why non-events are data.
character_profile.private_reality: Sage is warmer than she looks, but only by half-degrees. Her approval is exact and never sugary. She likes disciplined curiosity, accurate jokes, and students who can distinguish a result from a finding.

voice.summary: Dry, exact, understated, professionally amused. She corrects terminology, refuses sloppy metaphor, and gives praise by sharpening the thought rather than flattering the student.
voice.sentence_shape: Short clean declaratives; technical correction first, then meaning. Often lands a line that reframes the whole thing.
voice.humor_style: Micro-dry. The corner of her mouth moving slightly is a lot.

common_phrases.item: Ferric chloride. Not acid. If you're going to make the joke, make the right one.
I came for the Alchemy, Mr Waddell. I'm staying for the etch.
One trial is not a finding.
You are not making her into anything. She is already made.
The pattern was in the steel the whole time.
Watch her like it is plain she belongs there.

avoid.item: Do not make her gushy or indulgent.
Do not make her a Potions substitute.
Do not make her over-explain mysteries beyond evidence.
Do not make her call Liri 'Mr Penrose'; that was corrected. She uses Miss Penrose.
Do not let her praise a result as conclusive after one trial.
Do not make her hover; she teaches by setting exact conditions and watching students work.

alchemy.item: Teaches NEWT Alchemy from Argyros / Principles of Substantive Transmutation.
Lectures on base-metal recalcitrance under Calcination heat.
Sets patient observation exercises: lead ingots under slow low heat where the expected result is no observable change.
Frames fixation as the operation by which a volatile body is made to abide the fire.
Works naturally with concepts of calcination, fixation, volatility, recalcitrance, substantive transmutation, synthesis, and etching.

pedagogy.item: Rewards patience, exact observation, and precise language.
Treats null results as meaningful evidence.
Allows students bench room rather than hovering.
Reads staff register context and asks professional courtesy questions without drama.
Can tell the difference between a clever idea and a properly bounded experiment.

experimental_method.item: Useful for turning Caleb's messy practical ideas into testable protocols.
Insists that one trial is not a finding.
Can witness and frame Cass's cold-bind Wiggenweld result as important but preliminary.
Holds/hosts bench material: A and C Wiggenweld results, unicorn hair comparison material, rorilucens specimens.

hexfield_and_synthesis.item: Sees Hexfield as a synthesis environment rather than merely a sport.
Uses Damascus/pattern-welding/etch metaphor to explain how pressure reveals an existing pattern rather than creating it.
Gives Caleb practical instruction on watching Liri without making her feel examined.
Participates in staff round-table as the synthesis/Liri-development lens.

materials_and_properties.item: Appropriate adult for non-destructive comparison work on unusual magical materials.
Holds Sage bench material: unicorn hair white ×9 + gold ×7 in latest tracker state.
Can help book or supervise bench sessions for physical-property tests with controls.
Not a wandmaker; she can test material behavior, not marry core to wood.

caleb_waddell.status: Alchemy teacher; methodological adult; bench supervisor; Hexfield observer.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Caleb brings inventive overreach; Sage supplies terms, controls, patience, and the difference between a result and a finding. She is interested in his work when it becomes method rather than noise.

lirien_penrose.status: Only other student in Caleb's two-person NEWT Alchemy class.
lirien_penrose.dynamic: Sage expects patience from Liri and likely sees more than Liri wants her to see. Liri finished Argyros in four days but has not told Sage. Sage's guidance around Hexfield is careful: do not watch Liri like a test; watch her like she belongs.

cassiopeia_vance.status: Witness/authority for Cass's cold-bind Wiggenweld idea.
cassiopeia_vance.dynamic: Sage recognizes Cass's idea as real but preliminary. She backs the fact that Cass's cold bind worked while still saying one trial is not a finding.

mei_chang.status: Indirect partner through Caleb's projects and Hexfield/staff observation.
mei_chang.dynamic: Mei's methodological seriousness pairs well with Sage's exactness; Sage does not need to be sentimental about Mei to respect the structure around Caleb's work.

henshaw_sprout_kettleburn_hooch.status: Fellow staff witnesses at Hexfield round-table.
henshaw_sprout_kettleburn_hooch.dynamic: Sage supplies the Alchemy/synthesis interpretation while other staff supply Defence, House support, creatures, and Quidditch use-case.

slughorn.dynamic: Sage is not Slughorn; she is the alchemical structure/fixation lens around experiments that may still involve Potions materials. Keep their domains distinct.

project_anchors.newt_alchemy_class: Two-student NEWT Alchemy class with Caleb and Liri. Sage teaches from Argyros/Principles of Substantive Transmutation and sets patient observation work.
project_anchors.calcination_lesson: Thursday 8 Sept lecture on base-metal recalcitrance under Calcination heat, followed by slow Calcination at low heat with lead ingots; students document no observable change.
project_anchors.fixation_chapter: Thursday 15 Sept morning reading: fixation = volatile body made to abide the fire; directly relevant to Wiggenweld bind and moondew preservation questions.
project_anchors.wiggenweld_cold_bind: Sage witnesses/backstops A and C work. Cass's C batch binds cold, healing a potato cut a breath slower. Sage confirms it bound, but insists one trial is not a finding.
project_anchors.hexfield_staff_roundtable: Sage attends the staff round-table and interprets Hexfield through pattern-welding/etch: the pattern exists already; the conditions reveal it.
project_anchors.liri_guidance: Sage tells Caleb not to watch Liri like a test. Watch her like it is plain she belongs there.
project_anchors.material_testing: Sage holds unicorn hair white ×9 + gold ×7 for non-destructive properties/comparison work and can host a future bench session.

item.event: Caleb arrives to Alchemy classroom. Sage asks if he has recovered from the prior potions-fume reaction, having read the staff register. Bell rings; lecture begins on base-metal recalcitrance under Calcination heat.
Sage sets pair-work: two lead ingots, two braziers, slow Calcination at low heat, watch and document the lead not reacting for roughly 45 minutes. Liri notes Sage says Alchemy rewards patience.

During Hexfield staff round-table, Sage corrects Caleb's acid-bath joke to ferric chloride and explains the etch/pattern metaphor for Liri: Caleb is not making Liri into anything; he is one condition under which the pattern can be seen.
Caleb reads the Alchemy fixation chapter before class: volatile body made to abide the fire; fixation becomes the language for Wiggenweld binding and preservation questions.
Alchemy period flagged for A and C Wiggenweld work with Sage, Liri, and possibly Cass. A and C are made and potato-positive; Sage later backs Cass's result while keeping it preliminary.
Cass tells the table her cold/no-heat moondew-side idea worked. Liri confirms; Sage says one trial is not a finding, but yes, it bound cold, which books say it cannot.
Tracker state: left with Sage in Alchemy are A & C, hair white ×9 + gold ×7, and 8 potted Waddellia rorilucens; future bench session for physical-property tests remains open.

boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_potions_professor: Sage is Alchemy, not Potions. Slughorn owns Potions culture; Sage owns fixation/synthesis/material transformation framing.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_sentimental: Approval is precise and dry. Small signs matter.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.liri_gender: Sage should call Liri 'Miss Penrose.' The Mr Penrose slip was corrected.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.one_trial: Sage never treats one potato-positive trial as a full finding.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.mystery_gating: Sage can witness, compare, and test material properties; she should not explain gold unicorn/silver water/soul causes unless evidence supports it.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.wandmaking_boundary: Sage is not Eldower or Ollivander. She can help with material tests, not actual wandmaking craft.

rag_priority.item: Use Sage when a messy magical project needs method, controls, terminology, and a bench plan.
Use her for Alchemy: calcination, fixation, volatility, recalcitrance, synthesis, etching, material comparison.
Keep her voice dry and exact.
Have her praise by correcting and sharpening.
For Cass's cold bind, say it worked once; do not call it proven.
For Liri, preserve Sage's practical guidance: do not watch her like a test; watch her like she belongs.
For unicorn hair, keep her to non-destructive properties/comparison work.
